questionText: Which TV series features 'Theme from New York, New York' style big band jazz in its opening?
funFact: The Sex and the City theme was originally just a placeholder — but test audiences loved it so much they kept it
backgroundSongName: Theme from New York, New York

questionText: Which TV series features 'My Friend of Misery' reworked into a haunting cello theme?
funFact: Nick Cave's 'Red Right Hand' became so synonymous with Peaky Blinders that fans can't hear it without picturing Tommy Shelby

questionText: Which TV series features a theme by Ramin Djawadi using cellos imitating an electric guitar?
funFact: Ramin Djawadi wrote the Game of Thrones theme using cellos because showrunners specifically said 'no flutes' — they wanted it dark, not fantastical

questionText: Which comedy series features a bass-heavy slap theme composed by Jonathan Wolff?
funFact: The Seinfeld bass theme was different for every episode — Jonathan Wolff re-recorded it each time to match Jerry's opening monologue rhythm

questionText: Which crime drama features 'Who Are You' by The Who as its opening theme?
funFact: CBS paid The Who a fortune for three different songs for three CSI shows — 'Who Are You', 'Won't Get Fooled Again', and 'Baba O'Riley'

questionText: Which sitcom features 'I Don't Want to Wait' by Paula Cole as its theme?
funFact: When Dawson's Creek went to streaming, the original theme was replaced due to licensing costs — fans were outraged and Netflix eventually restored it

questionText: Which TV series features a haunting piano theme by Ramin Djawadi based on Radiohead's 'Exit Music'?
funFact: Westworld featured player piano covers of modern songs — Radiohead, Soundgarden, and The Rolling Stones all played on a Victorian-era instrument

questionText: Which animated series features 'Superman' by Goldfinger in its opening credits?
correctAnswer: Tony Hawk's Pro Skater (video game, not TV)
options: Tony Hawk's Pro Skater (video game, not TV)
funFact: 'Superman' by Goldfinger became more famous from the Tony Hawk video game than from any radio play — it defined an entire generation's skateboarding soundtrack

questionText: Which TV drama features 'Breathe Me' by Sia in its legendary series finale?
funFact: The Six Feet Under finale, soundtracked by 'Breathe Me', is consistently voted one of the greatest TV endings of all time — it made Sia a household name years before 'Chandelier'

questionText: Which TV series features the song 'Suits Theme Song' by Ima Robot (aka 'Greenback Boogie')?
funFact: Suits became massively popular AGAIN when Meghan Markle married Prince Harry — Netflix reported it as one of their most-streamed shows in 2023

questionText: Which animated sitcom features Danny Elfman's iconic theme?
funFact: Danny Elfman wrote The Simpsons theme in just two days — it's been played over 700 times on TV, making it one of the most-heard compositions in history

questionText: Which HBO series features a jazz trumpet theme by Wynton Marsalis?
funFact: Treme is set in post-Katrina New Orleans and features real New Orleans musicians — it's the most musically authentic TV series ever made

questionText: Which TV series features 'Hells Bells' and other AC/DC songs prominently?
funFact: Beavis and Butt-Head made fun of music videos on MTV — but bands actually WANTED to be featured because it boosted their sales

questionText: Which Netflix true crime documentary features a chilling original score that became iconic?
funFact: Making a Murderer was filmed over 10 years by two filmmakers who maxed out their credit cards — it became Netflix's most-discussed show in 2015

questionText: Which TV series features 'Boss of Me' by They Might Be Giants?
funFact: 'Boss of Me' won the Grammy for Best Song Written for a TV show in 2002 — beating the Friends and Sopranos themes

questionText: Which medical drama features 'Superfreak' by Rick James in its pilot episode?
funFact: Scrubs' actual theme 'Superman' by Lazlo Bane was picked by creator Bill Lawrence because it captured the feeling of being overwhelmed on your first day at work

questionText: Which Danish political drama features a theme by Frans Bak that became internationally recognized?
funFact: Borgen was so popular internationally that it influenced real politics — several politicians cited it as inspiration, and it put Danish drama on the global map alongside The Killing

questionText: Which TV series features 'Immigrant Song' by Led Zeppelin in its opening?
funFact: 'Immigrant Song' was written after Led Zeppelin performed in Reykjavik, Iceland in 1970 — Robert Plant was inspired by Norse mythology and Viking history

questionText: Which TV series features the catchy theme 'Unbreakable' performed by the cast?
funFact: The theme song went viral as an auto-tune remix — it was intentionally made to sound like a viral news interview, parodying the 'Bed Intruder' song

questionText: Which TV crime drama features 'Ain't No Rest for the Wicked' by Cage the Elephant?
funFact: Cage the Elephant became famous partly through video game soundtracks — proving that gaming is now as important as radio for breaking new artists
backgroundSongName: Ain't No Rest for the Wicked

questionText: Which country is Enya from?
funFact: Enya has sold over 80 million records despite never touring and rarely doing interviews — she lives in a castle outside Dublin with dozens of cats

questionText: Which country is Bad Bunny from?
funFact: Bad Bunny was the most-streamed artist on Spotify in 2020, 2021, AND 2022 — three consecutive years, beating every English-language artist

questionText: Which country is Youssou N'Dour from?
funFact: Youssou N'Dour is considered the most famous singer in Africa — he also ran for president of Senegal in 2012

questionText: Which country is Angélique Kidjo from?
funFact: Angélique Kidjo has won 5 Grammy Awards and was named one of the 100 most influential people by Time magazine — Beyoncé calls her one of her biggest inspirations

questionText: Which country are Tinariwen from?
funFact: Tinariwen are Tuareg nomads who learned to play guitar in Muammar Gaddafi's military camps in Libya — they use music as a form of resistance

questionText: Which country is Nusrat Fateh Ali Khan from?
funFact: Nusrat Fateh Ali Khan is called the 'King of Qawwali' — Peter Gabriel brought him to Western audiences, and Jeff Buckley called him 'my Elvis'

questionText: Which country is MØ from?
funFact: MØ's real name is Karen Marie Ørsted — she co-wrote and performed 'Lean On' with Major Lazer, one of the most-streamed songs in history

questionText: Which country is Lukas Graham from?
funFact: Lukas Graham grew up in Christiania, Copenhagen's famous freetown commune — '7 Years' reflects his unconventional upbringing

questionText: Which country is Gipsy Kings from?
funFact: The Gipsy Kings are French-born of Spanish Romani descent — their flamenco-pop 'Bamboléo' became a worldwide hit and is played at every wedding

questionText: Which country is Aqua from?
funFact: 'Barbie Girl' was so popular that Mattel sued Aqua for trademark infringement — the judge dismissed the case saying 'the parties are advised to chill'

questionText: Who is the lead singer of Gorillaz?
funFact: Gorillaz is a 'virtual band' — the animated characters were created by artist Jamie Hewlett, but Damon Albarn from Blur provides the actual vocals

questionText: Who was the lead singer of Joy Division?
funFact: Ian Curtis died at age 23 in 1980 — the remaining members formed New Order and became one of the most influential electronic bands ever

questionText: Who is the drummer of Green Day?
funFact: Tré Cool's real name is Frank Edwin Wright III — he got the name 'Tré Cool' from his first band because 'très cool' means 'very cool' in French

questionText: Who is the DJ of Run-DMC?
funFact: Run-DMC's collaboration with Aerosmith on 'Walk This Way' in 1986 broke the barrier between rock and hip-hop and is considered a watershed moment in music history

questionText: Who is the lead singer of Muse?
funFact: Matt Bellamy has been named the greatest guitarist of the last 30 years by multiple magazines — he's known for playing with one hand while conducting with the other

questionText: Who is the bassist and primary songwriter of The Cure?
funFact: Robert Smith has worn the same smeared lipstick and wild hair look since the early 1980s — he once said he puts his makeup on in 10 seconds without a mirror

questionText: Who is the frontman of Rammstein?
funFact: Till Lindemann was a competitive swimmer before music — he qualified for the 1980 Olympics but East Germany boycotted, so he turned to poetry and eventually metal

questionText: Who was the original drummer of Gasolin'?
funFact: Gasolin' broke up in 1978 at the height of their fame — Kim Larsen said they quit because they wanted to stop while they were still friends

questionText: Who is the lead singer of Arcade Fire?
funFact: Arcade Fire recorded their debut 'Funeral' during a period where several band members lost family members — the raw grief is what gives the album its emotional power

questionText: Who is the lead singer of Talking Heads?
funFact: David Byrne's 'Big Suit' from the Stop Making Sense concert film was inspired by Japanese kabuki theatre — it made him look like a giant walking house

questionText: What is Kendrick Lamar's Pulitzer Prize-winning album?
funFact: Kendrick Lamar was the first non-jazz or non-classical musician to win the Pulitzer Prize for Music — the committee called DAMN. 'a virtuosic song collection'

questionText: What was Aretha Franklin's signature song that became a feminist anthem?
funFact: 'Respect' was originally written and recorded by Otis Redding — but Aretha's version so completely owned the song that even Redding admitted 'that girl took my song'

questionText: What instrument does Stevie Wonder play with his mouth?
funFact: Stevie Wonder signed with Motown at age 11 as 'Little Stevie Wonder' — his first hit 'Fingertips' was recorded live, and you can hear him lose his harmonica mid-song

questionText: What is Drake's birth name?
funFact: Drake started as an actor on Canadian teen drama 'Degrassi' — his character Jimmy Brooks was paralyzed in a school shooting, which he later referenced in songs

questionText: What is the name of Kanye West's fashion brand?
funFact: Kanye's first Yeezy sneaker with Nike sold out in 11 minutes — when he moved to Adidas, the deal was reportedly worth over $1 billion

questionText: What year did Taylor Swift famously switch from country to pop?
funFact: When Taylor Swift re-recorded her first six albums to own her masters, she caused the original versions to drop 90% in streaming — the most successful act of artistic reclamation in music history

questionText: What does the name 'Deadmau5' refer to?
correctAnswer: A dead mouse he found in his computer
options: A dead mouse he found in his computer
funFact: Joel Zimmerman found a dead mouse inside his computer, told friends about it online, and they started calling him 'that dead mouse guy' — the name stuck, with the '5' because 'deadmouse' was too long for a username

questionText: Which Beatles member was known as 'the quiet Beatle'?
funFact: George Harrison organized the Concert for Bangladesh in 1971 — it was the first major benefit concert in rock history and inspired Live Aid

questionText: What was the first music video played on MTV?
correctAnswer: Video Killed the Radio Star by The Buggles
options: Video Killed the Radio Star by The Buggles
funFact: MTV launched on August 1, 1981 at 12:01 AM — the irony of 'Video Killed the Radio Star' being the first video was entirely intentional

questionText: What famous collaboration did Elton John and Eminem perform at the 2001 Grammy Awards?
funFact: Eminem and Elton John's Grammy duet silenced critics who called Eminem homophobic — they became genuine friends, with Elton later helping Eminem get sober

questionText: Which Baz Luhrmann film features a modern pop soundtrack set in the 1920s?
funFact: Jay-Z executive produced The Great Gatsby soundtrack — mixing modern hip-hop with 1920s jazz, including a collaboration with Beyoncé and André 3000

questionText: Which film features 'Everybody's Talkin'' by Harry Nilsson?
funFact: 'Everybody's Talkin'' was a Fred Neil song that Harry Nilsson covered — it became the de facto theme of Midnight Cowboy, the only X-rated film to win Best Picture

questionText: Which Quentin Tarantino film features 'Stuck in the Middle with You' by Stealers Wheel during a torture scene?
funFact: Michael Madsen improvised the ear-cutting dance in Reservoir Dogs — Tarantino just told him to 'do something cool' while the song played
backgroundSongName: Stuck in the Middle with You

questionText: Which film features 'Where Is My Mind?' by Pixies in its final scene?
funFact: The buildings collapsing to 'Where Is My Mind?' is one of cinema's most iconic endings — the Pixies had no idea the song would become a cultural touchstone through the film

questionText: Which animated film features 'Shallow Hal' and 'Speechless' by Naomi Scott?
funFact: 'Speechless' was written specifically for the 2019 live-action Aladdin to give Jasmine more agency — it became an anthem for empowerment

questionText: Which Edgar Wright film features 'Harlem Shuffle' by Bob & Earl?
funFact: Baby Driver was edited to the music first — every car chase, gunshot, and footstep is perfectly synchronized to the soundtrack

questionText: Which Wes Anderson film features 'These Days' by Nico?
funFact: Wes Anderson's use of 'These Days' in Margot Tenenbaum's bathroom scene turned a forgotten 1967 Nico track into one of the most recognizable sad movie moments

questionText: Which film features 'Unchained Melody' by The Righteous Brothers in a pottery scene?
funFact: The pottery scene in Ghost made 'Unchained Melody' a hit AGAIN in 1990 — 25 years after its original release, it re-entered the charts worldwide

questionText: Which superhero film features 'Sunflower' by Post Malone and Swae Lee?
funFact: 'Sunflower' spent 33 weeks in the Billboard Top 10 — one of the longest runs ever, largely because people kept rewatching Spider-Verse

questionText: Which Denis Villeneuve film features a score using traditional Middle Eastern instruments by Hans Zimmer?
funFact: Hans Zimmer chose Dune over Christopher Nolan's Tenet — the first time he ever turned down Nolan. He invented new instruments specifically for the Dune score

questionText: Which Danish band had the biggest international hit with 'Barbie Girl'?
funFact: 'Barbie Girl' reached #1 in 14 countries simultaneously and sold over 8 million copies — making Aqua the most commercially successful Danish pop act internationally

questionText: Which Danish artist grew up in Christiania and had a global hit with '7 Years'?
funFact: The '7 Years' music video was filmed in one continuous take in a studio — the aging makeup transformation took 12 hours to apply

questionText: Who was the guitarist of Gasolin' known for his innovative playing style?
funFact: Franz Beckerlee was influenced by Hendrix and was considered one of the best guitarists in Scandinavia — he rarely gave interviews and remained mysterious throughout his career

questionText: Which country is Olivia Rodrigo from?
funFact: Olivia Rodrigo's 'drivers license' broke the Spotify record for most streams in a single day when it was released — she was only 17

questionText: Which country is The Weeknd from?
funFact: The Weeknd dropped out of high school at 17 and was homeless in Toronto before his music went viral on YouTube — he went from sleeping on friends' couches to headlining the Super Bowl

questionText: What was Tupac Shakur's mother's connection to the Black Panther Party?
correctAnswer: She was an active member who was acquitted of 156 charges
options: She was an active member who was acquitted of 156 charges
funFact: Tupac's mother Afeni Shakur was a Black Panther who was pregnant with him while standing trial for conspiracy — she represented herself in court and was acquitted

questionText: Who is the lead vocalist of Imagine Dragons?
funFact: Dan Reynolds has been open about his struggles with depression — he created the LoveLoud festival to support LGBTQ+ youth, particularly in the Mormon community he grew up in

questionText: Which Ryan Gosling film features a jazz-heavy soundtrack by Justin Hurwitz?
funFact: Justin Hurwitz was Ryan Gosling's college roommate at Harvard — Gosling learned to play piano for real for La La Land, practicing for 3 months before filming

questionText: Which TV show features 'Brand New Key' style folk music reworked as a creepy children's theme?
funFact: The American Horror Story theme was created by mixing backwards piano, distorted strings, and creaking sounds — composer César Dávila-Irizarry wanted it to feel like 'a nightmare you can't wake up from'

questionText: Which sitcom features the catchy 'Big Bang' theme by Barenaked Ladies?
funFact: The Barenaked Ladies recorded the full version of the Big Bang Theory theme in one take — it covers 14 billion years of history in under two minutes

questionText: Which TV drama features 'Paint It, Black' by The Rolling Stones in its opening?
funFact: 'Paint It, Black' has been used in more Vietnam War-era TV and film than almost any other song — despite being written about a breakup, not war
